t:
Sustained rainfall continues across West Kenya, with peak expected between 23rd–24th March. Rivers are full and rising, with River Nyando already overflowed, keeping the situation critical.

Impacts:
Schools closed, roads flooded and impassable, businesses disrupted and high risk of displacement in
exp: This post provides meteorological information about sustained rainfall in West Kenya and flooding impacts including school closures and road blockages. It is factual weather and disaster reporting with no hate speech elements.

It adds that emergency teams, including Kenya Red Cross, are conducting rescue operations, while re
exp: This post reports on an official government statement about River Nyando flooding and emergency response teams. It is standard news reporting about disaster management with no hate speech content.

t:

Areas identified as at risk include Kibera, parts of Highrise, Lang’ata, South C, Mbagathi Way, and Madaraka.
